data.text: Subject eligibility evaluation (review of inclusion/exclusion criteria) will be checked. Subjects may have a prescreening more than 28 days prior to first administration of trial treatment.
All visits and assessments from Cycle 2 onwards may be performed 3 days to accommodate unforeseen delays, holidays, or vacations.

Performed within 14 days of the last dose of trial treatment, reason for treatment discontinuation should be recorded. If the subject discontinues from the trial at a scheduled visit, the End of Treatment assessment can be performed on that day.

30-Day Safety Follow-up visit, performed at 30 ± 3 days after the last trial treatment for all subjects who discontinue trial treatment permanently, including subjects who have completed an End of Treatment visit.

Subjects who withdraw from the treatment for reasons other than PD have additional visits for tumor assessments. A ± 7 day time window is permitted for additional follow-up visits until 9 months, then ± 14 days thereafter. Reasons for study termination should be recorded if this visit is the last visit for the subject. Recording of any new anticancer therapy will be made (a tumor assessment is mandatory before initiating the new therapy).

Survival follow-up, to be performed every 3 months 2 weeks at clinic visit or by telephone contact. Subjects’ survival information will be collected. Any new anticancer therapy given to the subject until death should be recorded.
For subjects who have a prescreening ICF, this information will be recorded in prescreening.
Full medical/disease history will be recorded at Screening/Baseline.
Provision of a fresh or archived pretreatment tumor biopsy (excluding fine needle aspiration); please refer to the laboratory manual for details.
Optional tumor biopsy. Subjects need to sign a separate consent.
Only for women of childbearing potential, including those who have had a tubal ligation.

Assessments can be repeated at the Investigator’s discretion at unscheduled visits. To assess the safety and tolerability of the IMP. Serum pregnancy test is permitted if urine pregnancy test is not available at the site.
Not necessary if thoracic CT is performed as part of the screening RECIST Version 1.1 tumor assessment.
Only if last assessments were performed 7 days prior to End of Treatment visit.

Vital signs (pulse rate, systolic and diastolic blood pressure, body temperature, respiratory rate) are to be taken after at least 5 minutes rest in seated position. If vital signs and ECGs are taken at the same time, vital signs should be taken prior to each ECG recording.

On Cycle 1, Day 1 and on Cycle 2, Day 1, an ECG will be recorded predose (within 60 minutes prior to dose) and at 4 hours ± 12 minutes postdose. ECG recordings must be performed before PK sampling time points on days where both assessments are performed. The ECGs must be taken as triplicate 12-lead resting ECGs within 2 minutes after minimum of 10 minutes rest in supine position. Start of resting time, ECG and PK sampling time must be recorded in the electronic Case Report Form.

Single ECG predose only on Cycle 3, Day 1 (within 30 minutes prior to dose), after 5 minutes rest in supine position. After Cycle 3, single ECGs will be performed after 5 minutes rest in supine position every third cycle through Cycle 15 (i.e., Cycle 3, 6, 9, 12, and 15) or as clinically indicated. Beginning at Cycle 15, single ECGs will be performed after 5 minutes rest in supine position every odd cycle thereafter (Cycles 15, 17, 19, 21, etc) or as clinically indicated.
Single ECGs will be performed after 5 minutes rest in supine position.

Hemoglobin, hematocrit, red blood cell count, white blood cell count, differential white blood cells, platelets, and coagulation (prothrombin time, activated thromboplastin time, and International normalized ratio).
Laboratory tests for screening should be within 7 days prior to Cycle 1 Day 1. Laboratory assessments will be performed by the local laboratory.

Blood urea nitrogen, urea, creatinine, alanine aminotransferase, aspartate aminotransferase, gamma-glutamyl transpeptidase, total bilirubin (including direct fraction if total bilirubin abnormal), lipase, total amylase, total protein, albumin, alkaline phosphatase, creatinine clearance, sodium, potassium, calcium, magnesium, glucose, and serum cystatin (for sites where the test is available).

Complete tumor assessment of all lesions by radiographic modality (using RECIST Version 1.1). CT or MRI of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis to evaluate disease in these locations. At Screening/Baseline, brain imaging by MRI with IV contrast enhancement; MRI may be performed without contrast enhancement, if contrast is contraindicated. If MRI is not clinically feasible, CT of the brain with IV contrast enhancement may be used. For subjects with brain metastases detected at Screening/Baseline only, subsequent brain imaging by MRI with IV contrast enhancement; MRI may be performed without contrast enhancement, if contrast is contraindicated. If MRI is not clinically feasible, CT of the brain with IV contrast enhancement may be performed; CT may be performed without contrast enhancement, if contrast is contraindicated. Additional anatomic areas should be investigated in case of suspicion of presence of metastases based on signs and symptoms of individual subjects.

Subjects will be assessed every 6 weeks following the Cycle 1, Day 1 visit until 9 months and every 12 weeks thereafter according to RECIST Version 1.1 until disease progression, death or withdrawal of consent (Investigator and independent read).
Only if last tumor assessment was performed ≥ 6 weeks within the first 9 months, or ≥ 12 weeks after 9 months prior to End of Treatment visit.

Sparse PK sampling (total of 6 samples) will be performed predose, at 1.5 hours postdose and at 4 hours postdose on Cycle 1, Day 1 and on Cycle 2, Day 1. The actual time of PK sample collection must be recorded. On days when PK samples are to be drawn, subjects should be instructed to attend the trial visit in a fasted state, with no breakfast and prior to taking their dose of tepotinib.
Predose only; collection will only be performed after signed consent for PGx testing has been obtained and only if local regulations allow.
On-treatment ctDNA samples to be taken at predose at Cycle 3, Cycle 5 and EoT only.

PROs (EQ-5D-5L, EORTC QLQ-C30 and QLQ-LC13): the questionnaires will be completed every 6 weeks from Cycle 1, Day 1 until 9 months and every 12 weeks thereafter until disease progression, death or withdrawal of consent. At all visits indicated including EoT and Safety Follow-up, every effort should be made to have the questionnaires completed by the subject prior to the initiation of any other trial activities (including RECIST Version 1.1 assessments) or active treatment and prior to any contact with the Investigator.

Consider provision of a FFPE tumor sample (a fresh or archived pretreatment tumor biopsy [excluding fine needle aspiration]) in case the subject is enrolled based on assays with appropriate regulatory status. Please refer to the respective laboratory manual for details.
To be taken at predose. Blood sample collection for exploratory biomarker research only if local regulations allow.

The blood sample is to be taken at screening if the subject is enrolled based on assays with appropriate regulatory status and no blood sample was provided during prescreening for determination of MET status in plasma.
To be performed only for subjects with a history of congestive heart failure or if clinically indicated.

data.name: Informed Consent
data.text: Signed, written informed consent by subject or legal representative prior to any trial-specific screening procedure;

data.name: Age and Sex
data.text: Male or female, ≥ 18 years of age (or having reached the age of majority according to local laws and regulations, if the age of majority is > 18 years of age [i.e., ≥20 years of age in Japan]);

data.name: Measurable Disease
data.text: Measurable disease by IRC in accordance with RECIST Version 1.1;

data.name: ECOG Performance Status
data.text: Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group Performance Status (ECOG PS) of 0 or 1;

data.name: Female Contraception Requirements
data.text: A female subject is eligible to participate if she is not pregnant, not breastfeeding, and at least 1 of the following conditions applies: a. Not a woman of childbearing potential as defined in Appendix VIII OR b. A woman of childbearing potential who agrees to use a highly effective contraception (i.e., methods with a failure rate of less than 1% per year) as detailed in in Appendix VIII of this protocol 2 weeks before start of first dose of study treatment, during the treatment period and for at least 4 weeks after the last dose of study treatment. Women of childbearing potential must have a negative pregnancy test (β-HCG test in serum) prior to enrollment.

data.name: NSCLC Diagnosis
data.text: Histologically or cytologically confirmed advanced (locally advanced or metastatic) NSCLC (all types including squamous and sarcomatoid);

data.name: Prior Therapy Lines
data.text: Treatment naïve patients in first-line or pretreated patients with no more than 2 lines of prior therapy;

data.name: MET Alterations
data.text: Subjects with MET alterations, namely: METex14 skipping alterations in plasma and/or tissue, as determined by the central laboratory or by an assay with appropriate regulatory status will, be enrolled into the trial. For these subjects, sufficient tumor tissue and/or plasma is requested to allow additional testing; MET amplification only in plasma defined by a positive LBx test, as determined by the central laboratory or by an assay with appropriate regulatory status; Based on the outcome of the interim analysis in 12 LBx selected subjects: MET amplification only in tissue defined by a positive TBx with a gain of at least 4 copies of the MET gene, as determined by the central laboratory or by an assay with appropriate regulatory status.

data.name: Symptomatic Brain Metastases
data.text: Subjects with symptomatic brain metastases who are neurologically unstable, and/or have required an increase in steroid dose within 2 weeks and/or have received prior stereotactic radiosurgery/gamma knife within 2 weeks and/or other prior treatment for brain metastases within 4 weeks prior to the start of therapy. Subjects with leptomeningeal disease are ineligible;

data.name: Unresolved Toxicity
data.text: Any unresolved toxicity Grade 2 or more according to National Cancer Institute-Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (NCI-CTCAE) Version 4.03, from previous anticancer therapy;

data.name: Need for Transfusion
data.text: Need for transfusion within 14 days prior to the first dose of trial treatment;

data.name: Brain Metastasis as Only Measurable Lesion
data.text: Subjects who have brain metastasis as the only measurable lesion.

data.name: EGFR Activating Mutations
data.text: Subjects with characterized EGFR activating mutations that predict sensitivity to anti-EGFR therapy;

data.name: ALK Rearrangements
data.text: Subjects with characterized ALK rearrangements that predict sensitivity to anti-ALK therapy;

data.name: Prior Anticancer Therapy
data.text: Prior chemotherapy, biological therapy, radiation therapy, hormonal therapy for anti-cancer purposes, targeted therapy, or other investigational anticancer therapy (not including palliative radiotherapy at focal sites) within 21 days prior to the first dose of trial treatment.

data.name: Inadequate Hematological Function
data.text: Inadequate hematological function: Hemoglobin < 8.5 g/dL; Neutrophils < 1.5 × 109/L; Platelets < 100 × 109/L.

data.name: Inadequate Liver Function
data.text: Inadequate liver function: total bilirubin > 1.5 × upper limit of normal (ULN); AST/ALT > 3 × ULN; For subjects with liver metastases: total bilirubin > 1.5 × ULN, AST/ALT > 5 × ULN

data.name: Inadequate Renal Function
data.text: Inadequate renal function: Severe renal impairment as evidenced by: Serum creatinine ≥1.5 × ULN; Creatinine clearance (CrCl) < 30 mL/min calculated by the Cockcroft-Gault formula (24 hour CrCl might be requested by the Investigator for confirmation, if calculated CrCl is < 30 mL/min. In such case, subjects with 24 hour CrCl < 30 mL/min should be excluded) CrCl (mL/min) = [140 – age (year) × weight (kg)] {× 0.85 for female subjects} / 72 × serum creatinine (mg/dL)

data.name: Prior HGF/c-Met Pathway Agents
data.text: Prior treatment with other agents targeting the HGF/c-Met pathway

data.name: Impaired Cardiac Function
data.text: Impaired cardiac function: a. Left ventricular ejection fraction < 45% defined by echocardiography (a screening assessment not required for subjects without a history of congestive heart failure unless clinically indicated); b. Serious arrhythmia; c. Unstable angina pectoris; d. New York Heart Association heart failure class III and IV; e. Myocardial infarction within the last 12 months prior to trial entry; f. Symptomatic pericardial effusion

data.name: Uncontrolled Hypertension
data.text: Hypertension uncontrolled by standard therapies (not stabilized to < 150/90 mmHg)

data.name: History of Other Neoplasm
data.text: Past or current history of neoplasm other than NSCLC, except for curatively treated non-melanoma skin cancer, in situ carcinoma of the cervix, or other cancer curatively treated and with no evidence of disease for at least 5 years

data.name: Gastrointestinal Conditions
data.text: Medical history of difficulty swallowing, malabsorption, or other chronic gastrointestinal disease, or conditions that may hamper compliance and/or absorption of the test product

data.name: Major Surgery
data.text: Major surgery within 28 days prior to Day 1 of trial treatment

data.name: Infections
data.text: Known infection with human immunodeficiency virus, or an active infection with hepatitis B or hepatitis C virus

data.name: Other Medical/Psychiatric Conditions
data.text: Substance abuse, active infection, or other acute or chronic medical or psychiatric condition or laboratory abnormalities that might increase the risk associated with trial participation at the discretion of Investigators

data.name: Hypersensitivity
data.text: Known hypersensitivity to any of the trial treatment ingredients

data.name: Legal Incapacity
data.text: Legal incapacity or limited legal capacity

data.name: Investigator Discretion
data.text: Any other reason that, in the opinion of the Principal Investigator, precludes the subject from participating in the trial

data.name: Participation in Other Clinical Trial
data.text: Participation in another clinical trial within the past 30 days.

data.name: Primary Efficacy Objective for Part 1: Cohort A
data.text: To assess the efficacy of tepotinib in subjects with advanced (locally advanced or metastatic) non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC), as per objective response (confirmed complete response [CR] or partial response [PR]) determined according to Response Evaluation Criteria in Solid Tumors (RECIST) Version 1.1, based on independent review in: Subjects tested positive for METex14 skipping alterations, regardless of MET amplification status; Subjects tested positive for METex14 skipping alterations based on liquid biopsy (LBx), regardless of MET amplification status; Subjects tested positive for METex14 skipping alterations based on tumor biopsy (TBx), regardless of MET amplification status.

data.name: Secondary Efficacy Objective
data.text: To further assess the efficacy of tepotinib in terms of: Objective response as per Investigator; Duration of response by Independent Review Committee (IRC); Duration of response by Investigator; Objective disease control as per IRC; Objective disease control as per Investigator; Progression free survival as per IRC; Progression free survival as per Investigator; Overall survival.

data.name: Secondary Safety and Tolerability Objective
data.text: To assess the tolerability and safety of tepotinib in terms of: Number of subjects with treatment-emergent adverse events (TEAEs) and deaths; Number of subjects with markedly abnormal clinical laboratory tests; Number of subjects with markedly abnormal vital signs, electrocardiogram (ECG), and physical examination.

data.name: Secondary Pharmacokinetic Objective
data.text: To assess pharmacokinetics (PK) of tepotinib and its metabolite(s)

data.name: Secondary Health-Related Quality of Life Objective
data.text: To assess Health-Related Quality of Life.

data.name: Exploratory Biomarker Objective
data.text: To explore a possible link between biomarkers of mesenchymal-epithelial transition factor (c-Met) pathway activation, other relevant oncogenic pathways in plasma, serum and tumor tissue, and the activity of tepotinib

data.name: Exploratory QT/QTc Interval Objective
data.text: To explore the QT/QTc interval concentration relationship based on Cycle 1, Day 1 and Cycle 2, Day 1 data

data.name: Exploratory Exposure-Response Objective
data.text: To investigate the exposure-response relationship.

data.name: Exploratory Efficacy Objective for Part 1: Cohort B Subgroups
data.text: To assess efficacy of tepotinib in subjects with advanced (locally advanced or metastatic) NSCLC, in: Subjects with gene copy number (GCN) gain ≥ 4 and < 6 irrespective of LBx test result; Subjects with GCN gain ≥ 6 irrespective of LBx test result.

data.name: Plasma Concentrations for Population PK Analysis
data.text: Plasma concentrations from sparse PK sampling to allow population PK analysis. Sparse PK sampling will be performed predose, at 1.5 hours postdose and at 4 hours postdose on Cycle 1, Day 1 and on Cycle 2, Day 1. Endpoints include, but are not limited to, CL/f and V/f derived from plasma concentrations. Population PK analysis will be reported separately. The PK of tepotinib and its metabolites will be assessed using a population PK analysis approach.

data.name: Supportive treatment

data.name: Anticonvulsants

data.text: Continue tepotinib treatment at a lower dose level until the AE related to tepotinib recovers to ≤Grade 2 or to baseline values

data.name: Unresolved Toxicity Exclusion
data.description: No unresolved toxicity Grade 2 or more from previous anticancer therapy
data.text: Any unresolved toxicity Grade 2 or more according to NCI-CTCAE Version 4.03, from previous anticancer therapy

data.name: End of Trial Definition
data.description: All subjects discontinued trial drug and two-thirds of subjects in each cohort have died
data.text: The end of the trial is defined as the time point at which all subjects have discontinued trial drug and two-thirds of the subjects in each of the 3 cohorts, respectively, have died.

data.name: Negative Pregnancy Test
data.description: Women of childbearing potential must have a negative pregnancy test prior to enrollment
data.text: Women of childbearing potential must have a negative pregnancy test (β-HCG test in serum) prior to enrollment

data.name: Prescreening to Screening Transition
data.text: Subject must have confirmed MET alteration status (METex14 skipping alterations or MET amplification) by central laboratory or assay with appropriate regulatory status. Subjects must sign a separate main ICF.

data.name: Screening to Treatment Transition
data.text: Subject must meet all inclusion criteria and no exclusion criteria. Screening procedures must be performed within 28 days before first dose. Time window from enrollment to first dose is 72 hours.

data.name: Treatment to End of Treatment Transition
data.text: End of Treatment visit will occur within 14 days of last dose of trial treatment upon disease progression (RECIST 1.1), death, AE leading to discontinuation, or withdrawal of consent.

data.name: End of Treatment to Safety Follow-up Transition
data.text: 30-day follow-up visit should be performed at 30 ± 3 days after last dose of trial treatment for all subjects who discontinue trial treatment permanently, including subjects who have completed an End of Treatment visit.

data.name: Discontinuation for Disease Progression
data.text: Documented progression of the disease according to RECIST Version 1.1 requires withdrawal from tepotinib therapy.

data.name: Discontinuation for Adverse Event
data.text: Occurrence of AEs, if discontinuation of trial treatment is desired or considered necessary by the Investigator and/or the subject.

data.name: Discontinuation for Consent Withdrawal
data.text: Subject withdraws consent. Subjects may withdraw from the trial at any time without giving a reason.

data.name: Discontinuation for Pregnancy
data.text: Occurrence of pregnancy requires withdrawal from tepotinib therapy.

data.name: Discontinuation for Non-Compliance (Treatment Interruption Exceede
data.text: Non-compliance with administration of tepotinib. The maximum permitted period of continuous treatment interruption is 21 days.

data.name: Discontinuation for Prohibited Concomitant Drug
data.text: Use of a non-permitted concomitant drug where the predefined consequence is withdrawal from tepotinib.

data.name: Discontinuation for Initiation of Other Anticancer Treatment
data.text: Initiation of any other anticancer treatment (including radiotherapy, surgery, hormonal therapy) requires withdrawal from tepotinib therapy.

data.name: Discontinuation for Therapeutic Failure
data.text: Therapeutic failure (i.e., oncologic emergency due to serious tumor progression or serious side effect) requiring urgent additional therapy.

data.name: Dose Reduction Modification
data.text: Dose reduced from 500 mg to 250 mg once daily (standard dose reduction after protocol Version 8.0). If subject does not tolerate 250 mg once daily or AE does not resolve following treatment interruption, permanent treatment discontinuation should be discussed with the Sponsor.

data.name: Treatment Interruption Modification
data.text: Maximum permitted period of continuous treatment interruption is 21 days. Following a treatment interruption, subjects can be re-challenged at the initial dose level of 500 mg.

data.name: Dose Re-escalation
data.text: Following dose modifications, the dose of tepotinib may be increased again to 500 mg at the discretion of the Investigator.

data.name: Treatment Discontinuation to Additional Follow-up (Non-PD)
data.text: Subjects who withdraw from treatment for reasons other than PD have additional visits for tumor assessments. Assessments continue until disease progression, withdrawal of consent, or death.

data.name: Cohort B Futility Interim Analysis Decision
data.text: Based on outcome of the interim analysis in 12 selected subjects tested positive for MET amplification by LBx, if at least 3 out of 12 subjects respond (CR + PR), enrollment continues to enrich LBx cohort to 60 subjects and 2 additional TBx analysis sets may be started.

data.name: Premature Trial Termination by Sponsor or Health Authorities
data.text: The clinical trial may be terminated prematurely or suspended at the request of Health Authorities or if new safety or efficacy information leads to an unfavorable risk benefit judgment for tepotinib. The Sponsor may discontinue the trial due to futility, if it becomes unjustifiable for medical or ethical reasons, for poor enrollment, or because of discontinuation of clinical development.

data.name: Exclusion Criterion Occurrence During Treatment
data.text: Occurrence of an exclusion criterion which is clinically relevant and affects the subject's safety, if discontinuation is considered necessary by the Investigator and/or Sponsor.

data.name: Participation in Another Clinical Trial
data.text: Participation in another clinical trial will be considered withdrawal from the trial.

data.name: Disease Progression
data.description: Subject discontinues treatment upon documented disease progression according to RECIST Version 1.1.

data.name: Adverse Event Leading to Discontinuation
data.description: Subject discontinues treatment due to adverse event considered unacceptable by the Investigator and/or subject.

data.name: Withdrawal of Consent
data.description: Subject may withdraw from the trial at any time without giving a reason. Subjects who are withdrawn for any reason must not re-enter this trial.

data.name: Death
data.description: Subject death during the trial.

data.name: Treatment Interruption Exceeds 21 Days
data.description: Non-compliance with administration of tepotinib where the maximum permitted period of continuous treatment interruption of 21 days is exceeded.

data.name: Intolerance to Lowest Dose Level
data.description: If a subject still does not tolerate the 250 mg once daily dose, or the AE does not resolve following treatment interruption, permanent treatment discontinuation should be discussed with the Sponsor.

data.name: Pregnancy
data.description: Occurrence of pregnancy requires immediate withdrawal from tepotinib therapy.

data.name: Premature Trial Termination by Sponsor
data.description: The Sponsor may discontinue the trial due to futility, unfavorable risk-benefit, poor enrollment, discontinuation of clinical development, or withdrawal from market for safety reasons.

data.name: Study Completion - End of Trial
data.description: End of trial defined as when all subjects have discontinued trial drug and two-thirds of the subjects in each of the 3 cohorts have died. Final analysis including efficacy and safety will be conducted.

data.name: Lost to Follow-up
data.description: Subject lost to follow-up requires withdrawal from tepotinib therapy.

data.name: Safety Concern Exit
data.description: Any events that unacceptably endanger the safety of the subject require withdrawal from tepotinib therapy.

data.name: Cohort B Futility Interim Analysis
data.description: Futility analysis in initial 12 LBx-tested subjects in Cohort B. At least 3 out of 12 subjects must respond (CR + PR) to continue enrollment. Determines enrichment of LBx cohort to 60 subjects and start of exploratory TBx cohorts.

data.name: Tumor Assessment Response Decision (First 9 Month
data.description: Tumor assessments every 6 weeks following Cycle 1 Day 1 until 9 months per RECIST 1.1. Decision to continue or discontinue treatment based on disease progression.

data.name: Dose Modification Decision
data.description: Decision to temporarily interrupt treatment, reduce dose to 250 mg, or permanently discontinue based on AE severity and recovery to Grade 2 or less or baseline values.

data.name: Primary Analysis Decision Cohort A and B
data.description: Primary analysis conducted once all subjects in each primary analysis set have been treated for at least 6 months, died, or prematurely discontinued trial treatment.

data.name: Rescreening Decision
data.description: Rescreening is permitted only for subjects with confirmed METex14 alterations or MET amplification if they do not meet one of the other inclusion or exclusion criteria and after discussion with the Sponsor.
